name: Prepare and publish semantic releases from the UI

rounds.prompt: Implement a complete UI-first agent-assisted semantic release workflow, building on Refine durable operations. In System > Releases, let the user initiate Prepare Release, explicitly choose major/minor/patch, preview current and proposed versions, and start an agent-operated release preparation that analyzes completed Goals and commits since the prior release, updates all applicable version-bearing files and lockfiles, writes release notes and updates story/runbooks/migration or other affected docs, identifies breaking changes, and runs deterministic release gates. Show live persisted stages, agent activity, outputs, failures, and retry/resume actions in the UI. Preparation must produce a normal reviewable candidate rather than publishing directly. After the prepared change is reviewed/merged, expose an explicitly confirmed Publish Release UI action that preflights clean synchronized main, version/tag alignment, credentials and remote, then creates/pushes the semantic tag, observes deployment/package work, creates the GitHub release with notes/artifacts, and verifies publication. Publishing is externally mutating and must never occur without explicit confirmation. Implement shared backend release services and host abstractions so UI and any CLI surface reuse one implementation; make external Git/GitHub/deploy actions fully fakeable in tests. Persist operation progress under the runtime root so reload/reconnect preserves feedback. Add xtask release plan/check support where repository automation belongs, focused success/failure tests, UI coverage, and documentation/spec updates.

Correct the blocking release-workflow findings from review of round 1. Round 2 must carry forward candidate 456c6e3cbe564ffbf6c2dd7a8065811d19b0abd1 and preserve its useful UI, CLI, xtask, durable-operation, and documentation work while replacing the unsafe preparation/publication design.

1. Preparation must actually be agent-operated. Use Refine existing Goal/workflow service as the release-preparation execution boundary: Prepare Release should create and queue a normal visible Goal with an actionable prompt derived from the trusted ReleasePlan. That Goal tells the configured agent to analyze completed Goals and commits, update all applicable version/lock files, preserve established documentation formats, write release notes and breaking/migration guidance, and run xtask release-check. Return and persist the created goal_id and surface its real workflow state, activity/logs, candidate branch/commit, failures, and review link in Releases. Do not label deterministic shell edits as release-agent activity. Do not directly synthesize or append docs/story content in the release service.

2. The release preparation candidate must use the normal Goal-managed worktree/branch and review/approval path. All managed worktrees must remain under the target repository .git/refine-worktrees invariant. Remove the custom runtime-root releases/worktrees path. Retry/resume must never reset --hard, clean, overwrite, or discard review edits; retry the linked Goal/workflow stage or create an explicit new Goal only when safe.

3. Publication must use trusted server-side persisted preparation identity, not a PreparedRelease object supplied by the browser. The UI/API should submit a preparation operation id or goal id; the service resolves and validates its persisted candidate/version/notes. Reject tampering and candidates that were not prepared and approved through this workflow.

4. Make publication compatible with the normal reviewed merge. Require clean synchronized main on its configured upstream remote/branch, require the approved preparation commit to be an ancestor of main, verify the merged version and tag alignment, and tag synchronized main HEAD (the merge commit), not require main HEAD to equal the pre-merge candidate commit. Discover the configured remote instead of hardcoding origin.

5. Make external publication truly resumable and verifiable with a fakeable stage-level host. Complete all non-mutating preflight including auth before creating a tag. On retry, accept an existing local tag, remote tag, or GitHub release only when it resolves to the expected main commit, continue from the first missing stage, and reject conflicts. Observe relevant deployment/package workflow runs to a terminal conclusion or explicitly report that none are configured; do not mark verified merely because gh run list returned JSON. Verify the final GitHub release URL/tag after creation. Add focused fake-host tests for no-confirmation, tampered preparation identity, standard merge ancestry, configured non-origin remote, failure after tag push followed by successful resume, conflicting tag, failed workflow, and verified success. No test may push a real tag or create a real release.

6. Keep UI confirmation explicit for every publish attempt and show durable stage errors/recovery. Add UI/API tests proving the server loads trusted candidate state and the browser does not post arbitrary candidate paths/commits. Re-run generated CLI docs, JavaScript checks, fmt, strict Clippy, focused tests, the full unit suite, xtask release-check, and locked release build. No real release or tag is authorized.
